elements.sent-2411-256: In this section we will compute the irreducible representations of the Heisenberg group $H_n$.
elements.sent-2411-257: This is by no means a new result and it can be found for instance in the work of J.. Grassberger and G.
elements.sent-2411-259: We rely on the general method regarding semi-direct products of groups with the normal group being abelian, as it is described by Serre in section 8.2 of his book \cite{SerreLinear .}
elements.sent-2411-260: Indeed, we can realize $H_n$ as the semi direct product $\langle \alpha, \tau \rangle \rtimes \langle \beta \rangle$ since every element of $H_n$ can be expressed as $\beta^j \tau^k \alpha^i$, $0\leq i,j,k \leq n-1$ with $\tau = [\alpha,\beta]$ commuting with both $\alpha$ and $\beta$.
elements.sent-2411-261: As $H_n$ is isomorphic to $(\Z / n \Z)^2 \rtimes \Z/n\Z$ we denote by $\chi_{ij}$ and $\chi_s$ the irreducible characters of the first and the second component respectively, such that \begin{equation}\chi_{ij}(\alpha) = \zeta^i, \ \chi_{ij}(\tau) = \zeta^j, \ \chi_s (\beta) = \zeta^s, \quad 0\leq i,j,s \leq n-1,\end{equation} for a fixed primitive $n$-th root of unity $\zeta$.
elements.sent-2411-262: Applying the method in Serre's book, we can conclude that the irreducible representations of $H_n$ are those induced by the product $\chi_{ij}\otimes \chi_s$ for the indices $0\leq i,s \leq \gcd(n,j)-1$ and $ 0\leq j \leq n-1$.
